sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ingersoll Rand Officer Sells Shares
feed.summary: Michael J. Scheske, VP and Chief Accounting Officer of Ingersoll Rand, sold 1,477 shares of company stock for a total of $119,086 on December 1, 2025. Following this transaction, Scheske directly owns 12,153 shares of Ingersoll Rand. This insider trading activity was reported in a SEC Form 4 filing.

feed.title: Free cash flow per share of Ingersoll Rand Inc. – FWB:5GD
feed.summary: This page on TradingView.com displays the financial metric "Free cash flow per share" for Ingersoll Rand Inc., traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange under the symbol FWB:5GD. It allows users to view the period, value, and percentage change for this specific financial data point. The page is designed for investors and analysts tracking the financial performance of industrial manufacturing companies.

feed.title: │Chapter: Appendix C: EAF Steel Facilities
feed.summary: │This article, "Appendix C: EAF Steel Facilities," provides a detailed list of Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) steel facilities across the United States. It includes information on company names, facility locations (city and state), and their respective annual production capacities in tons, distinguishing between combined EPA and AIST data, AIST-only data, and stainless/specialty carbon steel production. The appendix also briefly mentions small companies and non-operating companies in the EAF steel sector.

feed.title: Insulet Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Insulet Corp. (PODD) stock rose 1.83% to $155.19 on Friday, outperforming its competitors and the broader market. Despite this daily gain, the stock remains significantly below its 52-week high of $367.65. This performance comes as the S&P 500

feed.title: Sell Rating on Bristol-Myers Squibb Due to Cobenfy Trial Challenges and Uncertain Sales Forecast
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Terence Flynn maintained a Sell rating on Bristol-Myers Squibb with a $36.00 price target. This decision is based on significant challenges in the Phase 3 ADEPT-2 trial for Cobenfy, including trial execution irregularities, data exclusion, and delays, pushing the completion to late 2026. These issues raise concerns about Cobenfy's approval prospects for Alzheimer’s Disease Psychosis and its future sales forecasts, contributing to a cautious outlook on the stock.

feed.title: Teradyne stock hits all-time high at 191.98 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) stock has reached an all-time high of $191.98, marking a 65.21% increase over the past year and a 133.61% surge in six months, driven by strong investor confidence and advancements in technology and automation solutions. The stock's performance has led to multiple analyst upgrades and increased price targets, with analysts citing favorable changes in test revenue, strong guidance, and demand for custom ASIC testing related to AI. Despite appearing overvalued according to InvestingPro data, Teradyne maintains a "GOOD" financial health score and has announced a quarterly cash dividend, reflecting positive sentiment from analysts.

feed.title: KMI Financial Statements & Charts - Kinder Morgan Inc - Ordinary Shares - Class P MacroTrends
feed.summary: This page provides financial statements and charts for Kinder Morgan Inc (KMI), identified as Ordinary Shares - Class P, on MacroTrends via CMLViz. It displays real-time stock data including the current price and daily change, alongside key market indices.

feed.title: Salesforce jumps as AI tools help drive upbeat revenue outlook
feed.summary: Salesforce Inc. gave a stronger-than-expected revenue forecast, indicating increased customer spending on its Agentforce AI tools despite investor hesitancy regarding AI's long-term impact on legacy software companies. The company's shares rose significantly after its outlook and third-quarter results surpassed Wall Street estimates. This positive performance is largely attributed to the accelerating adoption of Agentforce and the Data 360 offering, along with a strategic $8 billion acquisition of Informatica.

feed.title: CapEx per share of Moderna, Inc. – DUS:0QF
feed.summary: This page provides financial data specifically focusing on the CapEx per share for Moderna, Inc. as traded on the Dusseldorf Stock Exchange under the ticker 0QF. It includes historical values and percentage changes for this metric over different periods. The page is part of TradingView's financial section, offering detailed statistics and ratios for the company.

feed.title: Salesforce posts Q3 revenue of $10.3 billion
feed.summary: Salesforce reported third-quarter revenue of $10.3 billion, marking an 11% year-over-year increase, or 12% in constant currency. The company's current remaining performance obligations (cRPO) for Q3 stood at $20 billion, an increase of 12% year-over-year, or 12% in constant currency. Despite these figures, Salesforce anticipates Q4 revenue to be between $10.70 billion and $10.75 billion, slightly below analysts' expectations of roughly $10.8 billion.

feed.title: Eversource Energy declares $0.7525 dividend
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.7525 per share, payable on September 29, 2023, to shareholders of record as of August 23, 2023. The ex-dividend date is August 22, 2023, and the dividend yield is 3.55%.

feed.title: General Dynamics board declares $1.50 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: General Dynamics announced a regular quarterly dividend of $1.50 per share, payable on February 6, 2026. The aerospace and defense company recently reported strong third-quarter earnings, exceeding analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, leading to increased price targets from Bernstein and UBS. General Dynamics also secured a significant contract with the U.S. Navy and expanded a partnership with Google Public Sector.

feed.title: Amcor PLC Updates CDI and Securities Issuance for November 2025
feed.summary: Amcor PLC announced updates to its Chess Depository Interests (CDIs) and other securities for November 2025. The company reported an increase in CDIs due to net transfers between CDIs and NYSE ordinary shares, and new share issuances from its merger with Berry. These changes reflect strategic adjustments in Amcor’s securities management, potentially impacting market positioning and stakeholder interests.

feed.title: STLD - Steel Dynamics Inc Latest Stock News & Market Updates
feed.summary: This page provides the latest news and updates for Steel Dynamics Inc. (STLD), a major U.S. steel producer and metals recycler. It includes recent announcements on acquisitions, debt offerings, leadership changes, dividends, new product launches, earnings reports, and trade rulings. The content highlights the company's operational developments, financial performance, and strategic initiatives, along with some key stock data.

feed.title: Solventum Corporation (SOLV) Stock Price | Live Quotes & Charts | NYSE
feed.summary: This article provides live stock quotes and charts for Solventum Corporation (SOLV), showing its current stock price and daily change. It also presents analyst ratings, though none are available, and notes the absence of earnings information. The page includes prompts for users to discover popular stocks and sign up for trade alerts.

feed.title: BMS flags site 'irregularities' in closely watched Alzheimer's psychosis trial
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) has reported "site-level data collection irregularities" in a crucial Phase II study evaluating its investigational drug, separate from its recent acquisition of Karuna's schizophrenia drug. This disclosure comes as analysts are closely monitoring the trial, which tests a muscarinic M1/M4 receptor agonist, for its potential in treating psychosis in Alzheimer's patients, a market that has seen recent disappointments. BMS stated these irregularities do not impact patient safety, and the trial remains on track to report comprehensive data in the first half of 2025.

feed.title: Quinn Emanuel in Fray as Bobcat Sues Caterpillar
feed.summary: Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan has filed a patent infringement lawsuit on behalf of Doosan Bobcat North America Inc. against Caterpillar Inc. in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Texas. Bobcat alleges systematic reverse-engineering of its compact equipment technologies and has also filed actions in German court and the Unified Patent Court of the European Union.

feed.title: Iron Mountain Executives Engage in Stock Transactions
feed.summary: Iron Mountain's President and CEO, William L Meaney, and EVP, GM Data Centers & ALM, Mark Kidd, recently engaged in significant stock transactions. Meaney sold over $5.7 million worth of shares, while Kidd sold $510,000 worth of shares, the latter under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. These transactions were reported in SEC Form 4 filings and detail their remaining holdings.

feed.title: Umicore - Transparency notification by Bank of America Corporation
feed.summary: Bank of America Corporation, through its subsidiary Merrill Lynch International, has notified Umicore of crossing the 3% legal threshold for direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments. As of November 26, 2025, Bank of America Corporation's total holding stands at 3.90%. This transparency notification details the breakdown of direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments following the transaction.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Raises Quarterly Dividends on Common Stock by 8% and Announces Quarterly Dividend on Preferred Stock; Announces Increased Common Stock Repurchase Authorization
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial announced an 8% increase in its quarterly cash dividend on common stock to $0.54 per share and authorized an increased common stock repurchase program of up to $2 billion, replacing the previous $1.5 billion authorization. The company also declared a quarterly dividend for its Series B Preferred Stock and plans to redeem all outstanding shares of it. These actions reflect the company's financial strength and commitment to shareholder returns.

feed.title: Cardinal Health (CAH) Stock Pulls Back From Record Highs: Latest News, Forecasts and Investment Outlook as of December 3, 2025
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) stock has experienced a pullback after a significant rally in 2025, with shares down 2.64% on December 3, 2025. Despite this recent weakness, the company has delivered strong year-to-date returns and robust Q1 FY 2026 results, driven by specialty pharmaceuticals and strategic acquisitions like Solaris Health. Analyst sentiment remains largely positive with "Buy" ratings and target price increases, though valuation concerns and execution risks are noted by more cautious investors, suggesting a potential consolidation phase for the stock.

feed.title: McKesson Corporation (MCK) Stock Today: Price Drop, Oncology Push and 2026 Outlook as of December 3, 2025
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation (MCK) experienced a stock price drop of nearly 3% on December 3, 2025, closing at $805.37, despite being a strong performer in U.S. healthcare throughout the year. The pullback is attributed to profit-taking, institutional portfolio adjustments, and investors processing new guidance and oncology initiatives. The company is actively reshaping its portfolio by spinning off its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and focusing on oncology, specialty drugs, and tech-enabled services, with analysts generally bullish on its long-term prospects.

feed.title: Kimco Realty® Achieves ‘A3’ Credit Rating from Moody’s
feed.summary: Kimco Realty® announced it has achieved an ‘A3’ credit rating with a stable outlook from Moody’s Ratings, making it one of the few REITs with A-level ratings from the three largest agencies. Moody’s cited Kimco's high-quality, grocery-anchored shopping center portfolio and prudent financial structure as reasons for the upgrade. This rating affirms the company's operational excellence, strong access to financial markets, and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Boston Scientific Stock (BSX) on December 3, 2025: Insider Sales, EU Device Win and Strong 2026 Price Targets
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed snapshot of Boston Scientific (BSX) stock on December 3, 2025, highlighting recent insider sales, a significant EU device approval for its Farapoint pulsed field ablation catheter, and strong analyst price targets for 2026. It also recaps the company's Q3 2025 earnings, growth drivers, valuation, and risks, indicating a premium-valued medtech leader with bullish prospects tempered by insider selling and high valuation.

feed.title: Citigroup Stock Hits New 52‑Week High as Bank Redeems $1.5 Billion in Preferreds – Is C Still a Buy for 2026?
feed.summary: Citigroup stock hit a new 52-week high of $106.72 on December 3, 2025, following a 3.4% daily gain and strong year-to-date performance. The surge was propelled by the redemption of $1.5 billion in Series W preferred stock, a smaller-than-usual managing director class reflecting cost discipline, and strategic hires in prime brokerage. These actions strengthen Citi's ongoing transformation, raising questions about whether the stock, which is up 50-55% in 2025, still represents a buy for 2026 given its current valuation and an expected 10-11% return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) target.

feed.title: Newmont Stock (NEM) After December 3, 2025: Record Free Cash Flow, Job Cuts and Insider Selling – Is There Still Upside?
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NEM) experienced a standout 2025, with its stock more than doubling in value after achieving record free cash flow, notably $1.6 billion in Q3 2025. The company is undergoing significant restructuring, including job cuts and a CEO transition, following its Newcrest acquisition, all while benefiting from near-record gold prices. Despite a recent insider stock sale and warnings about Q4 free cash flow, analysts generally maintain a bullish outlook, though with modest near-term upside expectations.

feed.title: Will Strong Q3 Beat And Pipeline Updates Change Amgen's (AMGN) Future Earnings Narrative?
feed.summary: Amgen reported stronger-than-expected Q3 results in late November 2025, driven by significant Repatha sales growth and strong contributions from Prolia and rare disease drugs, leading to raised full-year 2025 guidance. Investors are also optimistic about updates for obesity candidate MariTide and lung cancer therapy Imdelltra, which are seen as key future growth drivers. These developments are expected to reshape Amgen's investment narrative, although concerns about intensifying drug pricing pressures remain.

feed.title: Does PADCEV Keytruda Bladder Cancer Win Reshape The Bull Case For Pfizer (PFE)?
feed.summary: Pfizer and Astellas secured FDA approval for PADCEV/Keytruda as a perioperative treatment for cisplatin-ineligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer, enhancing Pfizer’s oncology portfolio. This approval strengthens Pfizer's oncology investment narrative, though overall expectations for the company remain balanced due to upcoming patent expirations, pricing pressures, and high debt. Despite this win, investors also weigh Metsera acquisition, cost-cutting, and elevated dividends against earnings dilution and leverage.

feed.title: Does Kellanova’s Snacks Focus Justify Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Kellanova's share price performance and valuation, considering its repositioning as a snacks-focused business. It evaluates the stock using Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Earnings ratios, suggesting it's undervalued by DCF but overvalued by PE ratio compared to a proprietary "Fair Ratio." The article encourages investors to use "Narratives" on Simply Wall St to form their own valuation based on individual assumptions.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern (NSC): Assessing Valuation After a Quiet Month of Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) shares have seen a quiet but steady climb over the past month, reaching $294.05, leading investors to assess its current valuation. While a "Narrative" from Simply Wall St suggests a 6% undervaluation with a fair value near $311.68 due to productivity initiatives and market share gains, "Another View" based on market ratios flags caution, with NSC trading at a P/E of 22.3x against a fair ratio of 18.2x. The article encourages investors to consider potential risks like storm-related costs and weaker export coal pricing that could impact margins.

feed.title: The Bull Case For S&P Global (SPGI) Could Change Following New AI Tie-Up And Debt Raise - Learn Why
feed.summary: S&P Global recently completed a US$1.00 billion debt raise and announced new AI data integrations with AWS, enhancing its financial flexibility and data innovation. This move allows for potential acquisitions, buybacks, or further product investment, particularly in AI-driven tools. While the new debt introduces modest leverage and slightly raises the stakes if growth underperforms, investors should also consider execution risks associated with the AI initiatives and premium pricing.

feed.title: Baxter Defeats Suit Over Stable Value Fund In $4B 401(k) Plan
feed.summary: An Illinois federal judge dismissed a lawsuit against medical products company Baxter International Inc. concerning its $4 billion 401(k) plan. The suit alleged the company violated federal benefits law by retaining a stable value fund that performed poorly, but the judge ruled the presented data did not adequately capture the fund's long-term performance.

feed.title: BofA, UBS, Goldman Halt Festivities After Deadly Hong Kong Blaze
feed.summary: Bank of America Corp., UBS Group AG, and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. have canceled festive celebrations in Hong Kong as a mark of respect for the victims of the city's deadliest blaze in eight decades. BofA canceled its biggest year-end event for global markets clients, while UBS canceled its mid-December Christmas party and Goldman Sachs postponed several departmental events. This decision reflects a somber mood among global finance firms in the region.

feed.title: Protesters call on DTE for transparency over proposed data center in Saline Township
feed.summary: Residents in Saline, Michigan, protested outside DTE headquarters, demanding transparency regarding a proposed 1.4 gigawatt data center. They raised concerns about potential pollution, increased electrical costs, and environmental impact. Activists are urging the Michigan Public Service Commission to scrutinize contracts between DTE and the developer, Related Digital, following a lawsuit settlement that allowed the project to proceed.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial raises quarterly dividend by 8%, ups buyback
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial, Inc. announced an 8% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.54 per share and authorized a new $2 billion share repurchase program. The company also declared a dividend for its Series B Preferred Stock and confirmed plans to redeem all outstanding shares of this preferred stock. These actions follow robust earnings results and positive analyst revisions to price targets.

feed.title: General Dynamics Board Declares Dividend
feed.summary: The board of directors of General Dynamics declared a regular quarterly dividend of $1.50 per share on the company's common stock. This dividend is payable on February 6, 2026, to shareholders of record on January 16, 2026. General Dynamics is a global aerospace and defense company with over 110,000 employees and $47.7 billion in revenue in 2024.

feed.title: Eversource Energy declares quarterly dividend of $0.7525 per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: Eversource Energy has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.7525 per share, payable on December 31, 2025. This announcement follows recent financial impacts, including Connecticut regulators rejecting the sale of its Aquarion Water Company, impacting the company's financial outlook and leading to several analyst downgrades and revised price targets. Eversource reaffirmed its 2025 non-GAAP earnings guidance despite these challenges.

feed.title: News | CoStar World News for Dec. 4
feed.summary: This article provides a global real estate news roundup for December 4, 2025, highlighting key developments in Japan's hotel market, JPMorgan Chase's plans for a new London headquarters, an LVMH-backed fund's investment in a French Riviera hotel, growth forecasts for German commercial investment, and an architectural project in Canada addressing loneliness. It also covers rising commercial real estate prices in the U.S., signaling a recovery trend.

feed.title: Everest Group names new chief actuary and risk officer By Investing.com
feed.summary: Everest Group, Ltd. appointed Katy Bradica as Group Chief Actuary and Attila Kerényi as Group Chief Risk Officer, both effective early 2026. These appointments aim to strengthen the company's leadership amidst challenging market conditions. Bradica will lead global actuarial operations, while Kerényi will oversee Enterprise Risk Management.

feed.title: KMX INVESTORS: Contact Kirby McInerney LLP About Securities Class Action Lawsuit On Behalf of CarMax, Inc.
feed.summary: Kirby McInerney LLP reminds CarMax, Inc. investors of a securities class action lawsuit deadline on January 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges CarMax overstated growth prospects, as evidenced by a significant drop in retail and wholesale unit sales and net earnings per share in Q2 Fiscal Year 2026. This news led to an approximate 20.07% decline in CarMax's stock price.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets Inc. (CBOE) Stock Price | Live Quotes & Charts | BATS
feed.summary: This article provides live quotes and charts for Cboe Global Markets Inc. (CBOE), showing its current stock price and recent performance. It includes sections on analyst ratings, which are currently unavailable, and highlights the absence of earnings information. The content also features a promotional section about a "Bullish Fuel" indicator.

feed.title: Agilent Technologies Director Sells 2,600 Shares
feed.summary: Mikael Dolsten, a Director at Agilent Technologies, sold 2,600 shares of common stock for a total of $389,511 on December 1, 2025. The sale occurred at a weighted average price of $149.812 per share, and Dolsten now directly owns 4,973 shares in the company. This transaction was disclosed in an SEC Form 4 filing.

feed.title: Eisai and Biogen Present Long-Term LEQEMBI Data Indicating Potential Delay in Alzheimer's Disease Progression by Up to 8.3 Years
feed.summary: Eisai and Biogen presented new long-term data at the Clinical Trials on Alzheimer's Disease Conference for their drug LEQEMBI, suggesting it could delay Alzheimer's disease progression by up to 8.3 years in low-amyloid patients who start treatment early. The data also highlighted bioequivalence and a favorable safety profile for a new subcutaneous formulation, potentially increasing patient convenience and adherence. This news comes as LEQEMBI continues regulatory review for initiation treatment in the U.S. and Japan, facing scrutiny over potential side effects like Amyloid-Related Imaging Abnormalities (ARIA).

feed.title: Salesforce Inc earnings beat by $0.39, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Salesforce Inc (NYSE: CRM) reported Q3 EPS of $3.25, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.39, with revenue reaching $10.3B against an estimated $10.27B. The company issued Q4 2026 EPS guidance of $3.02-$3.04 and revenue guidance of $11.13B-$11.23B, both above consensus estimates. Salesforce stock has seen a decline of -4.81% in the last 3 months and -35.11% in the last 12 months, despite strong financial health according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Wells Fargo to lay off over 100 employees
feed.summary: Wells Fargo is set to lay off 103 employees at its South Jordan Creek Parkway location. The article, published by weareiowa.com, details the number of affected positions. This development was reported on December 3, 2025.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment vice chair Don Kornstein to retire in December By Investing.com
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment announced that Vice Chair Don Kornstein will retire on December 31, 2025, after 12 years of service, during which he played a significant role in the company's strategic initiatives, including the merger with Eldorado. His departure will reduce the board size from 12 to 11 members. The announcement comes amidst mixed analyst ratings and a 30% year-to-date decline in the company's stock, trading at $23.52, significantly off its 52-week high of $40.

feed.title: Exxon joins NY Brownfield program for Rochester site cleanup
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil is investigating and cleaning up a 33-acre contaminated former refinery site in Rochester, New York, which operated from 1866 to 1935. The company has enrolled the site in New York’s Brownfield Cleanup Program and will reimburse the state $250,000 for previous cleanup efforts. The project is expected to take around two years, with plans to redevelop the northern portion and maintain the southern portion as greenspace.

feed.title: T-Mobile's latest tactic to lure customers doesn't sit well with AT&T and Verizon
feed.summary: T-Mobile has introduced a new "Switching Made Easy" promotion via its T-Life app, using AI to scan AT&T or Verizon accounts and suggest T-Mobile plans. This tactic has drawn legal action and criticism from AT&T and Verizon, who claim T-Mobile's app collects excessive user data. T-Mobile has since adjusted its method, now asking customers to upload their bills instead of direct AI scanning.

feed.title: Total debt per share of Constellation Brands, Inc. Class A – LS:871918
feed.summary: This page displays financial data for Constellation Brands, Inc. Class A (LS:871918), specifically focusing on its total debt per share. It provides market information from Lang & Schwarz Tradecenter and indicates that the market is currently closed. While the data table is not populated in the provided text, the page is structured to present financial statistics.

feed.title: Netflix stock: key insider trims personal stake by 99%
feed.summary: Netflix stock slipped over 5% after cofounder and current chairman Reed Hastings trimmed his stake in the firm by 99%, selling 375,470 NFLX shares on December 1st. This significant insider selling is interpreted as a red flag, raising concerns about Netflix's long-term growth and strategic direction. However, Hastings's decision may also be influenced by Netflix's high forward P/E ratio and a challenging technical picture for the stock.
